heading: Internee of belligerent nation
label: 756 Internee of belligerent nation
text: Whoever, within the jurisdiction of the United States, aids or entices any person belonging to the armed forces of a belligerent nation or faction who is interned in the United States in accordance with the law of nations, to escape or attempt to escape from the jurisdiction of the United States or from the limits of internment prescribed, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than five years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 37 (, title V, § 7, ).June 15, 1917, ch. 3040 Stat. 223Section was divided. Remaining provisions relating to arrest appear in .section 3058 of this titleMinor changes in phraseology were made.
Amendments1996— substituted “five years” for “one year”.Pub. L. 104–1321994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $1,000”.Pub. L. 103–322
